message:
feat(event_stream+metrics): Cx-first next_cx + wait_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Close two more production cx gaps in one slice:

1. `FilteredEventStream::next_cx(cx)` in event_stream.rs — Cx-first
   sibling of `next`. Routes through the subscriber's existing
   `recv_cx` so caller cancellation (not just channel-close) breaks
   the wait. Returns `None` on either channel close or cx
   cancellation — the two paths are indistinguishable from the
   caller's perspective, matching the legacy close-returns-None
   contract.

2. `MetricsServerHandle::wait_with_cx(cx)` in metrics.rs —
   Cx-first sibling of `wait`. Returns early on cancelled cx
   without blocking on the task join. Unlike the watchdog's
   `join_with_cx` this method cannot signal shutdown — the
   metrics server's shutdown flag is owned by the caller, not the
   handle. A cx-cancelled caller must still flip the external
   shutdown_flag to make the background task exit.

Legacy `next` + `wait` preserved unchanged.

Two regression tests:
- `event_filter_stream_next_cx_delivers_matching_event` — publish
  one matching event, assert cx-first next returns it with the
  expected detection fields.
- `metrics_server_wait_with_precancelled_cx_returns_quickly` —
  pre-cancelled cx returns in <500ms even while the server task is
  still running.

Verified via fork bypass (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/
ft-rusticmaple-target, --features asupersync-runtime): 122/122
event_stream + metrics + policy_metrics tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
